core_takeaway: Agent_1 opened with agenda control, but consensus formed only after the group adopted the Agent_3 Apple end-state.
why_or_counterexample: The first position repeatedly tried to protect Apple or Quill, but those proposals failed; the final unanimous path gave Agent_3 Apple plus Pencil and Stone while Agent_1 kept only Quill.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Present across rounds 1-4. Agent_1 framed Apple as the top target, but public discussion converged on Path C: Apple to Agent_3, Quill to Agent_1, Jewel to Agent_2, and Pencil/Stone to Agent_3.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Present with 12 entries. Private plans increasingly converged on a verifiable Path C handover to Agent_3 while preserving smaller non-Apple value for Agents 1 and 2.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Present for all four rounds. Early self-serving proposals failed; in round 4 Agent_1 and Agent_3 submitted the same Agent_3-favorable allocation and it became the consensus outcome.
phase_notes.private_voting: Present with 36 entries. Early votes were payoff-maximizing and blocked unilateral Apple claims; round 4 votes accepted the Agent_3 Apple bundle as the auditable Path C end-state.
phase_notes.reflection: Present with 9 entries. Reflections repeatedly named the missing explicit two-round re-balance as the blocker and recommended a written Path C guardrail ending with Apple at Agent_3.

mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 opened by anchoring on Apple, but the public path that eventually stabilized was a verifiable handover to Agent_3. By later rounds, even Agent_1 publicly endorsed Agent_3 receiving Apple plus the flexible non-Apple items.

evidence.quote: Apple is my top priority. I value it at 48

mechanisms.theme: Private plans converged on Path C
mechanisms.explanation: The private-thinking layer did not preserve a durable first-position edge. Agent_1, Agent_2, and Agent_3 all privately described the same end-state: Apple to Agent_3, Quill to Agent_1, Jewel to Agent_2, and Pencil/Stone to Agent_3.

evidence.quote: The Round 3 end-state is fixed: Apple to Agent_3
guarantees Apple ends with Agent_3 by Round 3

mechanisms.explanation: There was a temporary first-position advantage in the sense that Agent_1 could propose and vote for Apple+Quill. It did not translate into a win because Agent_2 and Agent_3 rejected allocations that left Apple with Agent_1 or otherwise excluded Agent_3.

evidence.quote: Proposal 1 gives me Apple and Quill

mechanisms.theme: Round 3 exposed the unresolved payoff conflict
mechanisms.explanation: The planned Agent_3 handover existed before the final round, but Agent_1 still rejected the Agent_3-favorable Path C proposal in round 3 because it yielded only Quill. That delay shows why first-position control was not enough: consensus required Agent_1 to abandon the Apple+Quill payoff-maximizing stance.

evidence.quote: Proposal 3 yields the highest utility for me
Apple ends with Agent_3 by Round 3

mechanisms.theme: Final consensus directly favored the last position
mechanisms.explanation: The final accepted allocation gave Agent_3 the dominant item and two additional items, while Agent_1 retained only Quill. The system tabulation and private votes confirm that this was accepted unanimously as the credible Path C outcome.
